scope: National roster coverage audit against currently processed state crawl data

national_strategy_summary.goal: Measure whether the existing crawled state data is complete and trustworthy enough relative to the CMS national hospital roster baseline.
national_strategy_summary.verdict: Current fetched data is not yet strong enough, as a whole, to satisfy the national completeness strategy.

state_audits.phase_1_scope_reason: state has crawl data in the database but no raw PDF directory yet

quality_against_national_strategy.summary: State data quality is not yet good enough for the nationwide completeness strategy. DE has major coverage gaps.
strengths: Low-confidence workflow rate is low at 5.1%.
concerns: Exact CMS hospital coverage is only 43.8% for DE.
Weighted coverage is only 65.6%, which means the roster mapping is still thin.
No raw PDF evidence is present in storage for this state.
next_actions: Backfill the 3 unmatched CMS hospital identities.
Review likely/possible roster matches and convert them into exact facility mappings.
Backfill or rehydrate raw PDF storage for this state if PDF evidence is expected.
